seoTitle: Embed Draw.io in Confluence: 2026 Diagram Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to embed draw.io diagrams in Confluence in 2026, connect to Google Drive or OneDrive, and keep flowcharts as a single live visual source of truth.
intro: Static diagram exports break the moment your system changes, and jumping to separate design software slows down documentation. This guide shows how to embed a live draw.io diagram directly inside Confluence in 2026.

steps.heading: Open the diagram macro
steps.body: In edit mode, type `/draw.io` and select the diagram macro to open the editor window.

steps.heading: Choose your source
steps.body: Select Embed draw.io diagram to search and pull a graphic from another space, if one already exists.

steps.heading: Or connect a cloud file
steps.body: Use the external tabs to authorize and pull raw vector layouts from Google Drive or OneDrive.

steps.heading: Build your diagram
steps.body: Drag shapes from the left sidebar, link nodes with vector arrows, and add custom labels.

steps.heading: Configure viewer settings
steps.body: Adjust center constraints, lightbox triggers, and visible layers in the right-hand format drawer.

steps.heading: Publish the page
steps.body: Click Publish inside the draw.io editor, then click the main page Publish button to lock in the live dashboard.

faq.question: Will my diagram update everywhere it's embedded if I edit the master copy?
faq.answer: Yes, the canvas coordinates with the draw.io engine so layout components update automatically wherever the master graphic is hosted.

faq.question: Can I pull diagrams stored in Google Drive or OneDrive?
faq.answer: Yes, you can authorize a connection and pull raw .drawio vector files directly from either service.

faq.question: Can I reuse a diagram from another Confluence space?
faq.answer: Yes, the Embed draw.io diagram option lets you search and pull an existing graphic from another space.

faq.question: Can I control which layers are visible in the published diagram?
faq.answer: Yes, the format drawer lets you lock custom layer visibility states before publishing.
